decisions.summary: Track kickoff delivery via a tri-state `kickoffDelivered?: boolean` field on AgentState (state.json): undefined = legacy/pre-feature agent (never flag), false = spawned but kickoff not yet confirmed, true = kickoff confirmed delivered. Chosen over a separate file because the dashboard health classifier (determineHealthStatus) already reads state.json directly, so the signal is available with no extra I/O.

determineHealthStatus gains an EARLY stall rule that runs BEFORE the lastActivity-based logic: role==='work' && status==='running' && kickoffDelivered===false && (now - startedAt) > grace => { status: 'stalled' }. It must NOT rely on lastActivity (the heartbeat refreshes it, which is exactly why the ghost was misreported healthy). New status string is 'stalled', distinct from 'stuck'/'warning', so the flywheel/dashboard can target ghost agents specifically.

Stall grace window = 5 minutes from startedAt before flagging, to avoid false-positives on legitimately slow cold starts (large-model/pi warmup can take 30-60s+; the existing starting-grace is ~90-120s). 5 min is a comfortable margin above the 30s ready-poll + retry.

deliverAgentMessage changes its return type from Promise<void> to Promise<DeliveryResult> ({ ok: boolean; path: 'supervisor'|'channels'|'tmux'|'pi'|'codex'; failure?: string }). Additive and backward-compatible — existing void-ignoring callers are unaffected. Strict-mode (explicit 'supervisor'/'channels') still throws on failure; the result object describes the auto-mode outcome so spawn can verify which transport actually carried the kickoff.

Deacon/resume re-delivery sources the original kickoff from the already-persisted `<agentDir>/initial-prompt.md` artifact rather than rebuilding it from the vBRIEF/beads. Both spawn paths already write this file; re-reading it keeps the resume fix self-contained and avoids re-running buildWorkAgentPrompt.

Spawn verification: when ready=false OR delivery is not verified, retry kickoff once; if it still fails, do NOT silently markAgentRunning as a healthy running agent — record a failure (recordAgentFailure) with reason 'kickoff delivery failed' and emit an activity error so the stall surfaces immediately instead of becoming a ghost.

hazards.summary: Tri-state false-positive risk: agents already running before this ships have kickoffDelivered===undefined.
hazards.mitigation: The stall rule fires ONLY on explicit `=== false`, never on `undefined`. Legacy in-flight agents are never flagged.

hazards.summary: Slow cold starts (model warmup, pi/codex launch) can legitimately take 30-60s+ before kickoff is confirmed.
hazards.mitigation: Use a 5-minute grace from startedAt (D3) before the stall rule activates; align mentally with existing isStartingWithinGrace windows.

hazards.summary: Auto-mode tmux fallback in deliverAgentMessage returns ok=true even though sendKeys cannot confirm the agent actually received the text — receipt is unverifiable at the transport layer for tmux.
hazards.mitigation: Spawn verification must NOT treat tmux ok as a strong guarantee. Combine transport ok with the ready signal; supervisor ack is the only definitive success. The health classifier's kickoffDelivered===false secondary check still catches a tmux miss because kickoffDelivered is only flipped to true on a confirmed path.

hazards.summary: Resume re-delivery must be idempotent — must not double-deliver to an agent that DID receive its kickoff.
hazards.mitigation: Re-deliver from initial-prompt.md ONLY when kickoffDelivered===false; set kickoffDelivered=true after a confirmed re-delivery. kickoffDelivered===true/undefined resume paths keep the existing generic-continue behavior.

hazards.summary: Pi (FIFO writePiAgentPrompt) and Codex (inline `codex exec <prompt>`) deliver kickoff outside the deliverAgentMessage ladder; if kickoffDelivered is never set for them they could be falsely flagged stalled.
hazards.mitigation: Set kickoffDelivered=true on the Pi/Codex spawn paths immediately after their delivery step, OR scope the stall rule to harnesses that use the verifiable ladder. Prefer setting the flag in every spawn path so the classifier stays harness-agnostic.

hazards.summary: Adding a new 'stalled' health status string may be unstyled/unhandled by the dashboard frontend or a strict status union in contracts.
hazards.mitigation: Verify determineHealthStatus's return is a plain object (it is) and check the /api/health/agents consumer + frontend for any exhaustive switch on status; map 'stalled' to an existing severity if a strict union exists, otherwise add it.

sessionHistory.note: Initial planning session for PAN-1617 (ghost work agents: kickoff never delivered, misreported healthy)
